q: What does 有効応力 (effective stress) σ' represent in 土質力学 (soil mechanics)?
opts: 全応力 (Total stress) minus 間隙水圧 (pore water pressure)
全応力 (Total stress) plus 間隙水圧 (pore water pressure)
Just the 間隙水圧 (pore water pressure)
応力 (Stress) from 地下水 (groundwater) only
explain: 有効応力 (Effective stress) = σ - u (テルツァーギ (Terzaghi) 1925). It is the 応力 (stress) carried by the soil skeleton, controlling ALL mechanical behavior: せん断強さ (shear strength), 圧密 (consolidation) rate, and 液状化 (liquefaction) resistance.

q: What is the モール・クーロン (Mohr-Coulomb) failure criterion for drained conditions?
explain: Drained: τf = c' + σ'tanφ'. Both c' (粘着力 (cohesion)) and φ' (摩擦角 (friction angle)) are 有効応力 (effective stress) parameters from CD triaxial or drained direct shear tests. Undrained: τf = cu (φu=0).

q: In テルツァーギ (Terzaghi)'s 圧密 (consolidation) theory, what does Tv = 0.848 correspond to?
explain: Time factors: Tv=0.197 → U=50%. Tv=0.848 → U=90%. Tv=1.781 → U=99%. The formula: Tv = cv·t/H² where H is the drainage path length. Double drainage: H = half layer thickness.

q: 液状化 (Liquefaction) is most likely to occur in which soil type?
explain: 液状化 (Liquefaction) requires: (1) cohesionless soil (砂 (sand)/シルト (silt)), (2) saturated (below water table), (3) loose state (low Dr or low N), (4) cyclic loading (地震 (earthquake)). FL = CRR/CSR < 1.0 → liquefied.

q: What is the typical 摩擦角 (friction angle) φ' for loose to medium dense clean 砂 (sand)?
explain: 砂 (Sand): φ' = 28-32° (loose, rounded), 32-38° (medium dense), 38-45° (dense, angular). 粘土 (Clay): φ' = 20-30°. φ' depends on particle shape, size distribution, and state of compaction. From triaxial CD tests.

q: What happens to 有効応力 (effective stress) during undrained loading of saturated 粘土 (clay)?
opts: Excess pore pressure develops, 有効応力 (effective stress) doesn't change much short-term
explain: In undrained loading, volume change is prevented. Excess pore pressure develops to carry the applied load. 全応力 (Total stress) increases but σ' changes little initially. Over time (圧密 (consolidation)), u dissipates and σ' increases.

q: The compression index Cc is used for calculating:
explain: Sc = Cc·H·log(σ'f/σ'0)/(1+e0). Cc from oedometer test (slope of e vs log σ' curve in virgin compression). Cs = swelling/recompression index (used for OCR > 1). Typically Cc ≈ 0.009(LL-10) for remolded clays.

q: What does OCR > 1 indicate?
explain: OCR = σ'pc/σ'v0. OCR = 1: normally consolidated (NC). OCR > 1: overconsolidated (OC) — soil was previously under higher 応力 (stress) (e.g., erosion, past ice load). OC soils are denser and stronger than NC at same σ'v0.

q: What is Darcy's Law for flow through soil?
explain: v = k·i where v = seepage velocity (m/s), k = hydraulic conductivity (m/s), i = hydraulic gradient (dimensionless). Q = k·i·A. 粘土 (Clay): k ≈ 10⁻⁸-10⁻¹⁰ m/s. 砂 (Sand): k ≈ 10⁻³-10⁻⁵ m/s.

q: What is the general 支持力 (bearing capacity) equation (テルツァーギ (Terzaghi))?
explain: テルツァーギ (Terzaghi) (1943): qu = cNc + qNq + 0.5γBNγ. Nc, Nq, Nγ depend on φ'. For φ'=30°: Nc=30.1, Nq=18.4, Nγ=15.7. Allowable: qa = qu/FS. FS = 3 for static loads. Shape factors applied for non-strip footings.

q: What is the formula for P波 (P-wave) velocity?
explain: Vp = √(M/ρ) where M = constrained 弾性係数 (modulus) = K + 4G/3 = λ + 2G. Always Vp > Vs. In saturated soil, K ≫ G so Vp is dominated by bulk stiffness (~1500 m/s like water). Vs is unaffected by saturation (G same).

q: Why can't S-waves travel through water or liquefied soil?
opts: Shear 弾性係数 (modulus) G = 0 in fluids
explain: S-waves require shear stiffness G. Fluids (water, liquefied soil) have G = 0 — they cannot resist shear. Therefore Vs = √(G/ρ) = 0 in fluids. This is why S-waves don't travel through water and why Vs indicates 液状化 (liquefaction).

q: What is Vs30 (Vs30) and what is it used for?
opts: Average S波 (S-wave) velocity in top 30m for site classification
explain: Vs30 (Vs30) = 30/Σ(hi/Vsi) = time-average Vs for top 30m. Used for seismic site classification worldwide. Japan: 告示1457号. NEHRP classes: A(>1500), B(760-1500), C(360-760), D(180-360), E(<180) m/s.

q: Snell's Law for 屈折法 (seismic refraction) is expressed as:
explain: sin(θ)/V = constant (ray parameter). At critical angle ic: sin(ic) = V₁/V₂. Head wave travels along interface at V₂. Travel time: t = x/V₁ + 2h·cos(ic)/V₁. Crossover distance: xc = 2h√((V₂-V₁)/(V₂+V₁)).

q: In a PS検層 (PS logging) survey, which is measured?
opts: Both Vp and Vs vs depth in a ボーリング孔 (borehole)
explain: PS検層 (PS logging) (PS検層, JGS 3511) measures BOTH Vp and Vs at depth intervals in a ボーリング孔 (borehole). Surface source (sledgehammer), 3-component downhole receiver. Output: Vp(z), Vs(z) → Vs30 (Vs30), G0, E0, ν.

q: The small-ひずみ (strain) shear 弾性係数 (modulus) G0 from Vs is:
explain: G0 = ρ·Vs² where ρ = mass 密度 (density) (kg/m³), Vs = shear wave velocity (m/s). G0 in Pa. Example: ρ=1800 kg/m³, Vs=200 m/s → G0 = 1800×200² = 72 MPa. G0 = small-ひずみ (strain) (dynamic) 弾性係数 (modulus), larger than static G.

q: Rayleigh wave velocity VR in homogeneous material is approximately:
explain: VR ≈ 0.92·Vs for ν=0.25. Retrograde elliptical particle motion. In layered media, VR is dispersive (different frequency = different depth = different Vs). 表面波探査 (MASW) exploits this dispersion to build Vs profiles.

q: 屈折法 (Seismic refraction) CANNOT detect which condition?
opts: Velocity inversion (low-speed layer under high-speed layer)
explain: 屈折法 (Seismic refraction) requires velocity increasing with depth (V₂>V₁). A velocity inversion (low-speed layer beneath high-speed layer) creates a 'hidden layer' — the refraction method cannot detect it. Use reflection or tomography instead.

q: What is the typical Vs of soft 粘土 (clay)?
explain: Soft 粘土 (clay): Vs = 50-150 m/s (very soft). Dense 砂 (sand): 200-400. Soft 岩 (rock): 400-700. Hard 岩 (rock): >1000. Tokyo bay area soft alluvial clays often have Vs < 100 m/s → site class D → high amplification.

q: The H/V spectral ratio peak frequency in microtremor surveys corresponds to:
explain: H/V (Nakamura method 1989): H/V peak = f0 = site fundamental frequency (T0 = 1/f0). Related to average Vs and depth H: f0 ≈ Vs/4H. High H/V amplitude at f0 → strong site amplification expected.

q: In 標準貫入試験 (SPT), what does the N値 (N-value) represent?
opts: Number of blows per 300mm to drive the sampler
explain: N = number of blows to drive split-spoon sampler 300 mm (after 150 mm seating drive). Hammer: 63.5 kg, drop 760 mm. JIS A 1219. N60 = N×(ER/60) corrects for energy ratio. Japanese auto hammer: ER ≈ 72%.

q: The overburden correction factor CN for 標準貫入試験 (SPT) is applied to:
opts: Correct for ボーリング孔 (borehole) diameter
explain: CN corrects N60 to reference pressure (100 kPa). CN = √(Pa/σ'v0). (N1)60 = CN×N60. Deep samples appear lower N due to confinement. Correction brings all values to same confining 応力 (stress) for comparison.

q: In CPTu, what does Ic represent?
explain: Ic = √((3.47-logQt)²+(1.22+logFr)²) — Soil Behavior Type Index (Robertson 1990). Ic < 1.31: gravels. 1.31-2.05: clean sands. 2.05-2.60: silty sands. 2.60-2.95: sandy silts. 2.95-3.60: silty clays. >3.60: clays.

q: Wenner array apparent 比抵抗 (resistivity) formula is:
explain: Wenner: ρa = 2πa·(V/I) where a = electrode spacing, V = voltage between MN, I = injected current. K = 2πa (geometric factor). Larger a → deeper penetration (approximately 0.5a). Good for horizontal mapping.

q: PS検層 (PS logging) JGS 3511 measures:
opts: Dynamic P and S wave velocities in ボーリング孔 (borehole)
explain: PS検層 (JGS 3511): 3-component geophone moved down ボーリング孔 (borehole) in 1m steps. Surface hammer source on plank (P) or horizontal (S). First-arrival picks → interval Vp and Vs. Used for Vs30 (Vs30), G0=ρVs², seismic design.

q: The FL method for 液状化 (liquefaction) assessment uses:
explain: FL = CRR/CSR (道路橋示方書, Japanese Road Bridge Specification). FL < 1.0: liquefiable. FL = 1.0-1.5: marginal. FL > 1.5: safe. CRR from (N1)60cs or Vs. CSR = 0.65×(σv/σ'v)×(amax/g)×rd.

q: What is the critical angle in 屈折法 (seismic refraction)?
explain: ic = arcsin(V₁/V₂). At this angle of incidence, the refracted ray travels along the interface. Below ic: reflection occurs. At ic: head wave forms. Refraction works only when V₂ > V₁ (otherwise no ic exists).

q: What does a ボアホールカメラ (borehole camera) (孔内カメラ) primarily reveal that a core log alone cannot?
opts: 3D fracture orientation, aperture, and 地下水 (groundwater) inflow locations
explain: ボーリング孔 (Borehole) cameras provide visual images of fracture orientation (dip/strike), aperture (crack width), infill material, weathering grade, and exact depth of 地下水 (groundwater) inflow. Core samples lose orientation information during retrieval — the camera sees these features in place.

q: In a プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) test, what does the limit pressure PL represent?
opts: The elastic 弾性係数 (modulus) Em
The lateral ground failure pressure (ultimate resistance)
The 標準貫入試験 (SPT) N値 (N-value) equivalent
explain: PL = limit pressure is the asymptotic pressure at which the soil/岩 (rock) fails laterally during プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) inflation. It is analogous to 支持力 (bearing capacity) in the horizontal direction. PL ≈ 5.5×cu for soft 粘土 (clay). Used for pile lateral capacity and foundation design.

q: What is the K30 value in a 平板載荷試験 (plate load test) and what is the Japanese road base specification?
opts: K30 is depth of test; spec ≥ 150 MN/m³
K30 = 沈下 (settlement) at 300 kPa; spec ≥ 10 mm
K30 = pressure/沈下 (settlement) at 1.25mm; Japanese road base spec ≥ 110 MN/m³
K30 = 支持力 (bearing capacity) factor; spec ≥ 3.0
explain: K30 = subgrade reaction coefficient from 平板載荷試験 (plate load test). K30 = load at 1.25mm 沈下 (settlement) / 1.25mm, using a 300mm diameter rigid plate. Japanese road design specification (道路橋示方書): K30 ≥ 110 MN/m³ for road base layer. Higher K30 = stiffer subgrade.

q: 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) = 55% on a core log is classified as:
explain: 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) classification: 90-100% = Excellent (優良), 75-90% = Good (良), 50-75% = Fair (普通), 25-50% = Poor (不良), <25% = Very Poor (極めて不良). 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) = 55% falls in FAIR (50-75%). Tunnel support: steel sets + shotcrete likely needed. RMR will reflect this classification.

q: Ground Penetrating Radar (地中レーダー (GPR)) works BEST in which ground condition?
opts: Dry resistive 砂 (sand) or 礫 (gravel)
explain: 地中レーダー (GPR) signal is attenuated by high electrical conductivity. Saltwater, wet 粘土 (clay), and conductive soils absorb the EM energy — depth penetration drops to 0.5-1m. Dry 砂 (sand), 礫 (gravel), and dry 岩 (rock) are ideal (low conductivity) — depth up to 10-30m possible. Always check soil conductivity before deploying 地中レーダー (GPR).

q: In 地中レーダー (GPR), the depth formula d = v·t/2 uses two-way travel time because:
opts: The radar travels at double speed underground
The pulse travels DOWN to the reflector and BACK UP — hence ÷2 for one-way depth
The antenna moves twice per scan
explain: The radar pulse travels from surface antenna DOWN to the reflector, then reflects UP back to the same antenna. The recorded time t is two-way travel time (TWTT). Depth = (wave velocity × TWTT) / 2. Velocity v = c/√εr where εr is the soil dielectric constant.

q: Which investigation method is the ONLY standard way to directly measure the lateral stiffness (horizontal deformation 弾性係数 (modulus)) of soil or soft 岩 (rock)?
opts: プレッシャーメーター (Pressuremeter) test (孔内水平載荷試験)
平板載荷試験 (Plate load test)
explain: The プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) test (Ménard) is the ONLY standard in-situ test that directly measures horizontal 応力 (stress)-ひずみ (strain) behavior. It inflates a cylindrical probe against the ボーリング孔 (borehole) wall and records pressure vs. volume change. Em (プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) 弾性係数 (modulus)) is used directly in Ménard's 沈下 (settlement) formula for laterally loaded piles and flexible foundations.

q: What is the key difference between 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) and TCR (Total Core Recovery)?
opts: 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) counts pieces ≥100mm only (natural breaks); TCR counts ALL recovered core
TCR is for soft soil; 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) is for hard 岩 (rock)
岩盤品質指標 (RQD) is measured in % while TCR is in mm
explain: 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) = (Σ intact pieces ≥ 100mm natural breaks) / L × 100%. TCR = all recovered core / L × 100%. TCR ≥ 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) always. A core with many short pieces (all <100mm) could have TCR=100% but 岩盤品質指標 (RQD)=0%. Only natural fractures are counted for 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) — mechanical drill breaks are excluded.
